Applied_Species.reason: PRX1 was expressed/tested in yeast.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The paper states PRX1 was suggested to condense vindoline and catharanthine and discusses coupled product formation.

Primary_Substrate.reason: Vindoline and catharanthine were the fed substrates used to test vinblastine formation.

Primary_Product.reason: The paper discusses vinblastine-related coupling and anhydrovinblastine/leurosine-type products in this context.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: PRX1 was intended to directly form the target coupled product toward vinblastine.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: PRX1 is explicitly called a class III plant peroxidase.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All three listed findings are directly supported in the Results and Discussion.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: No in vivo vinblastine was achieved in yeast because PRX1 did not function properly.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: They used module integration, substrate feeding, confocal microscopy, and metabolite profiling.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Improper folding/expression blocking terminal conversion is directly supported.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The suggestion of finding a better homologue or using peroxisomal compartmentalization appears in the Discussion.
